description: §23.5.5 — a `_{}` INLINE foreign-code block governed by a NON-empty `capabilities=` set silences the presence-nudge lint. Same `_={ … }=` block as `inline-block-undeclared`, but the enclosing `<program lang="ts">` now declares `capabilities=[network("api.example.com")]`. Because the governing set is non-empty, W-FOREIGN-UNDECLARED-CAPABILITY stays silent — the ONLY structural difference from the twin fire case is the presence of the declaration. Proves the lint is presence-gated, not always-on for foreign code.
rationale: §23.5.5: the lint fires only when the governing capability set is EMPTY. Here the closest ancestor `<program>` declares a non-empty `capabilities=[network(...)]`, so the `_{}` block is governed by a non-empty set and the presence-nudge does NOT fire. The declaration is advisory (§23.5.1) — non-blocking — so no error accompanies it. No E-SCOPE-001, no E-FOREIGN-CAPABILITY-UNKNOWN.
